name: Dave Bailey
abstract: Samuel David Bailey (February 22, 1926 – December 28, 2023) was an American jazz drummer.

interviews.title: Dave Bailey and John McNeil Interview, 1959-10-25
Crescent City Crystals: Dave Bailey, Harold Christophe, Albert Delone, Sylvester Handy Interview, 1963-11-13
Gibson Brass Band: Joseph Bentley, Albert Miller, Ronald Cayette, Eddie Richardson, George Starling, George Davidson, Dave Bailey, Alphonse Boatner Spears, Carroll Blunt Interview

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: New Jazz at Carnegie
Benefit Concert for the Student Non-Violent Coordinating Committee

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Golson
about_quotes.evidence: He got Dave Bailey, the drummer, because they played together with Gerry Mulligan... I got Curtis Fuller, and I mentioned McCoy Tyner... So I called McCoy... joining Art Farmer and me in a group.
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Golson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Lou Donaldson
about_quotes.evidence: I went and got Dave Bailey, drummer... I got Herman Foster; I got Dave Bailey. And then, I got Peck Morrison... Then I got Ray Barretto... We were in the project. I got him on the date.
about_quotes.doc_title: Lou Donaldson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Art Farmer
about_quotes.evidence: Dave and I were working with Mulligan’s band at the time... I’d run into the drummer Dave Bailey on some jam sessions around town... we wound up using my brother and Dave Bailey
about_quotes.doc_title: Art Farmer — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Clark
about_quotes.evidence: We had – at one time we had Joe Benjamin on bass. One time we had Bill Crow on bass. One time we had Osie Johnson on drums and then Dave Bailey on drums.
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Clark — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Crow
about_quotes.evidence: years later when neither Mel Lewis or Dave Bailey were available for a Gerry Mulligan quartet job, and Gerry asked us for recommendations, I said I know Gus Johnson’s not doing anything right now
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Crow interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, October 18, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Roger Kellaway
about_quotes.evidence: And Dave Bailey was looking for a pianist... and he got me that, and that became two and a half years and ultimately led to wonderful relationships.
about_quotes.doc_title: Roger Kellaway interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 14, 1999
